mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in real personal consumption expenditures (RSAFS) reflects underlying consumer confidence and spending power, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month changes (Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 7.0%, labor market slack dissipates, triggering wage growth pressures and reducing recession risk premiums, which compresses equity discount rates and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative or flat when UNRATE crosses below 7.0% in walk-forward tests, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of falling federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) and rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals a synchronized monetary easing with strengthening real economic activity, which historically precedes equity bull runs by compressing risk premiums and boosting valuation multiples over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive SPX returns in >60% of historical instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained industrial capacity utilization above 75%, supply-side bottlenecks emerge, causing input cost inflation to outpace demand growth. This margin compression forces equity valuations downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or neutral when INDPRO exceeds its 10-year median, or the negative predictive power vanishes when controlling for CPIAUCSL inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor demand, which sustains household income and consumer spending, thereby supporting corporate revenue trajectories and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day SPX returns show no positive relationship with PAYEMS growth rates, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero over consecutive 12-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 800,000 units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, which historically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as market participants price in slower GDP growth.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive when HOUST drops below 800,000, or the signal's hit rate does not exceed 55% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in CPI (CPIAUCSL) and sustained zero federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates stagflationary pressure where monetary policy is constrained from tightening, forcing equity investors to demand higher risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative SPX returns in >50% of historical episodes, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from baseline when both series move in tandem.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime characterized by rising real GDP (GDPC1) and falling unemployment, equity markets experience a valuation rerating as earnings revisions accelerate, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat during confirmed GDP growth and unemployment decline episodes, or the signal's predictive power disappears when adjusted for FEDFUNDS changes.
